title: Hadoop MapReduce Protocol Abstraction
description:
The protocol directory is responsible for defining the communication protocol between the client and the Hadoop MapReduce framework. It provides a set of classes and interfaces that enable the client to submit jobs for execution and retrieve job status and results. The classes and interfaces in this directory collaborate to facilitate the communication between the client and the framework.

The ClientProtocol.java file is the key component of this directory, as it defines the ClientProtocol interface, which includes methods for submitting jobs, retrieving job status, and obtaining the latest profile for a job. The ClientProtocol interface is implemented by the JobClient class, which is the main entry point for submitting jobs and interacting with the Hadoop MapReduce framework.

The protocol directory plays a crucial role in the Hadoop MapReduce architecture, as it provides a standardized interface for communication between the client and the framework. This abstraction allows for easy integration of new clients and enables the framework to be used in a wide range of applications and scenarios.
